   217→check_subnet_conflict() {
   218→    local fixed_cidr="$1"
   219→    local pool_base="$2"
   220→
   221→    local fixed_net="${fixed_cidr%/*}"
   222→    if ip route | grep -qF "${fixed_net}/"; then
   223→        echo "Error: DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR ${fixed_cidr} conflicts with an existing route:" >&2
   224→        echo "  $(ip route | grep -F "${fixed_net}/")" >&2
   225→        return 1
   226→    fi
   227→
   228→    local pool_net="${pool_base%/*}"
   229→    local pool_two_octets="${pool_net%.*.*}"
   230→    if ip route | grep -qE "^${pool_two_octets}\."; then
   231→        echo "Error: DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE ${pool_base} overlaps with existing routes:" >&2
   232→        echo "  $(ip route | grep -E "^${pool_two_octets}\.")" >&2
   233→        return 1
   234→    fi
   235→    return 0
   236→}
   237→
   238→# Cross-check proposed CIDRs against sibling dockyard env files in the given
   239→# directory.  Prevents gen-env for instance B from picking a pool range that
   240→# shares a /16 with instance A's bridge CIDR (and vice versa), which would
   241→# cause a conflict when concurrent creates bring A's bridge up before B's
   242→# check_subnet_conflict runs.
   243→check_sibling_conflict() {
   244→    local fixed_cidr="$1"
   245→    local pool_base="$2"
   246→    local env_dir="$3"
   247→
   248→    [ -d "$env_dir" ] || return 0
   249→
   250→    local our_bridge_two our_pool_two
   251→    our_bridge_two="${fixed_cidr%.*.*}"     # e.g. "172.21" from 172.21.116.0/24
   252→    our_pool_two="${pool_base%/*}"
   253→    our_pool_two="${our_pool_two%.*.*}"     # e.g. "172.21" from 172.21.0.0/16
   254→
   255→    for sibling in "${env_dir}"/*.env; do
   256→        [ -f "$sibling" ] || continue
   257→        local sib_fixed sib_pool
   258→        sib_fixed=$(grep '^DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=' "$sibling" 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2) || true
   259→        sib_pool=$(grep '^DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=' "$sibling" 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2) || true
   260→        [ -n "${sib_fixed}${sib_pool}" ] || continue
   261→
   262→        if [ -n "$sib_fixed" ]; then
   263→            local sib_two="${sib_fixed%.*.*}"
   264→            # Our pool must not share a /16 with a sibling's bridge
   265→            if [ "$our_pool_two" = "$sib_two" ]; then
   266→                echo "Error: DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE ${pool_base} would overlap with sibling bridge ${sib_fixed} (from ${sibling})" >&2
   267→                return 1
   268→            fi
   269→        fi
   270→        if [ -n "$sib_pool" ]; then
   271→            local sib_pool_two="${sib_pool%/*}"
   272→            sib_pool_two="${sib_pool_two%.*.*}"
   273→            # Our bridge must not share a /16 with a sibling's pool
   274→            if [ "$our_bridge_two" = "$sib_pool_two" ]; then
   275→                echo "Error: DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR ${fixed_cidr} would overlap with sibling pool ${sib_pool} (from ${sibling})" >&2
   276→                return 1
   277→            fi
   278→            # Our pool must not share a /16 with a sibling's pool
   279→            if [ "$our_pool_two" = "$sib_pool_two" ]; then
   280→                echo "Error: DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE ${pool_base} would overlap with sibling pool ${sib_pool} (from ${sibling})" >&2
   281→                return 1
   282→            fi
   283→        fi
   284→    done
   285→    return 0
   286→}
   287→
   288→# ── Commands ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   289→
   290→cmd_gen_env() {
   291→    local NOCHECK=false
   292→    for arg in "$@"; do
   293→        case "$arg" in
   294→            --nocheck)  NOCHECK=true ;;
   295→            -h|--help)  gen_env_usage ;;
   296→            --*)        echo "Unknown option: $arg" >&2; gen_env_usage ;;
   297→        esac
   298→    done
   299→
   300→    # Determine output file
   301→    local out_file="${DOCKYARD_ENV:-./dockyard.env}"
   302→    if [ -f "$out_file" ]; then
   303→        echo "Error: ${out_file} already exists. Remove it first or set DOCKYARD_ENV to a different path." >&2
   304→        exit 1
   305→    fi
   306→
   307→    # Apply env var overrides or defaults
   308→    local root="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-/dockyard}"
   309→    local prefix="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-dy_}"
   310→    local pool_size="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"
   311→
   312→    # Generate random networks if not provided via env
   313→    local bridge_cidr="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-}"
   314→    local fixed_cidr="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-}"
   315→    local pool_base="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-}"
   316→
   317→    if [ -z "$bridge_cidr" ] || [ -z "$fixed_cidr" ] || [ -z "$pool_base" ]; then
   318→        local attempts=0
   319→        local max_attempts=10
   320→
   321→        while [ $attempts -lt $max_attempts ]; do
   322→            attempts=$((attempts + 1))
   323→
   324→            # Random /24 from 172.16.0.0/12 for bridge
   325→            local b2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))   # 16-31
   326→            local b3=$(( RANDOM % 256 ))        # 0-255
   327→            bridge_cidr="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-172.${b2}.${b3}.1/24}"
   328→            fixed_cidr="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-172.${b2}.${b3}.0/24}"
   329→
   330→            # Random /16 from 172.16.0.0/12 for pool (different second octet)
   331→            local p2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))    # 16-31
   332→            while [ "$p2" -eq "$b2" ]; do
   333→                p2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))
   334→            done
   335→            pool_base="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-172.${p2}.0.0/16}"
   336→
   337→            if [ "$NOCHECK" = true ]; then
   338→                break
   339→            fi
   340→
   341→            local env_dir
   342→            env_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${out_file}")" 2>/dev/null && pwd)" || env_dir=""
   343→            if check_subnet_conflict "$fixed_cidr" "$pool_base" 2>/dev/null && \
   344→               check_sibling_conflict "$fixed_cidr" "$pool_base" "$env_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
   345→                break
   346→            fi
   347→
   348→            # Reset for next attempt (only re-randomize what wasn't user-provided)
   349→            [ -n "${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-}" ] || bridge_cidr=""
   350→            [ -n "${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-}" ] || fixed_cidr=""
   351→            [ -n "${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-}" ] || pool_base=""
   352→
   353→            if [ $attempts -eq $max_attempts ]; then
   354→                echo "Error: Could not find non-conflicting subnets after ${max_attempts} attempts." >&2
   355→                echo "Provide explicit values via DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR, DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR, DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE." >&2
   356→                exit 1
   357→            fi
   358→        done
   359→    else
   360→        # All three provided explicitly — validate unless --nocheck
   361→        if [ "$NOCHECK" = false ]; then
   362→            check_subnet_conflict "$fixed_cidr" "$pool_base" || exit 1
   363→        fi
   364→    fi
   365→
   366→    # Validate all CIDRs are in RFC 1918 private ranges
   367→    check_private_cidr "$bridge_cidr" "DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR" || exit 1
   368→    check_private_cidr "$fixed_cidr"  "DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR"  || exit 1
   369→    check_private_cidr "$pool_base"   "DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"   || exit 1
   370→
   371→    # Conflict checks (unless --nocheck)
   372→    if [ "$NOCHECK" = false ]; then
   373→        check_prefix_conflict "$prefix" || exit 1
   374→        check_root_conflict "$root" || exit 1
   375→    fi
   376→
   377→    # Write config file
   378→    cat > "$out_file" <<EOF
   379→# Dockyard configuration
   380→# Generated by: dockyard.sh gen-env
   381→
   382→DOCKYARD_ROOT=${root}
   383→DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=${prefix}
   384→DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR=${bridge_cidr}
   385→DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=${fixed_cidr}
   386→DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=${pool_base}
   387→DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE=${pool_size}
   388→EOF
   389→
   390→    echo "Generated ${out_file}:"
   391→    echo ""
   392→    cat "$out_file"
   393→}
   394→
   395→cmd_create() {
   396→    local INSTALL_SYSTEMD=true
   397→    local START_DAEMON=true
   398→    for arg in "$@"; do
   399→        case "$arg" in
   400→            --no-systemd) INSTALL_SYSTEMD=false ;;
   401→            --no-start)   START_DAEMON=false ;;
   402→            -h|--help)    create_usage ;;
   403→            --*)          echo "Unknown option: $arg" >&2; create_usage ;;
   404→        esac
   405→    done
   406→
   407→    require_root
   408→
   409→    echo "Installing dockyard docker..."
   410→    echo "  DOCKYARD_ROOT:          ${DOCKYARD_ROOT}"
   411→    echo "  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX: ${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}"
   412→    echo "  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:   ${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR}"
   413→    echo "  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:    ${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR}"
   414→    echo "  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:     ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE}"
   415→    echo "  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:     ${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE}"
   416→    echo ""
   417→    echo "  bridge:      ${BRIDGE}"
   418→    echo "  service:     ${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
   419→    echo "  root:        ${DOCKYARD_ROOT}"
   420→    echo "  data:        ${DOCKER_DATA}"
   421→    echo "  socket:      ${DOCKER_SOCKET}"
   422→    echo "  user:        ${INSTANCE_USER}"
   423→    echo "  group:       ${INSTANCE_GROUP}"
   424→    echo ""
   425→
   426→    # --- Check for existing installation ---
   427→    check_private_cidr "$DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR" "DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR" || exit 1
   428→    check_private_cidr "$DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR"  "DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR"  || exit 1
   429→    check_private_cidr "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"   "DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"   || exit 1
   430→    check_root_conflict "$DOCKYARD_ROOT" || exit 1
   431→    check_prefix_conflict "$DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX" || exit 1
   432→    check_subnet_conflict "$DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR" "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" || exit 1
   433→
   434→    # --- 1. Download and extract binaries ---
   435→    local CACHE_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/.tmp"
   436→
   437→    # Version compatibility notes — do not upgrade these without reading:
   438→    #
   439→    # DOCKER_VERSION: static binary from download.docker.com/linux/static/stable.
   440→    #   Uses sysbox-runc as default runtime → the bundled runc 1.3.3 is never
   441→    #   called for sandbox containers, so this version does NOT trigger the
   442→    #   sysbox procfs incompatibility (nestybox/sysbox#973).
   443→    #
   444→    # SYSBOX_VERSION: 0.6.7.10-tc is a patched fork (github.com/thieso2/sysbox)
   445→    #   that adds --run-dir to sysbox-mgr, sysbox-fs, and sysbox-runc, allowing
   446→    #   N independent sysbox instances per host (each with its own socket dir).
   447→    #   SetRunDir() calls os.Setenv("SYSBOX_RUN_DIR", dir) and os.Args is scanned
   448→    #   directly in init() — bypasses urfave/cli v1 so --run-dir via runtimeArgs
   449→    #   works correctly for all three sockets including the seccomp tracer.
   450→    #   No wrapper script needed.
   451→    #   Fixed: https://github.com/thieso2/sysbox/issues/5
   452→    #   Distributed as a static tarball (no .deb, no dpkg dependency).
   453→    #   0.6.7.10-tc is the first release with an aarch64 static tarball.
   454→
   455→    # --- Detect CPU architecture ---
   456→    local ARCH
   457→    ARCH=$(uname -m)
   458→    case "$ARCH" in
   459→        x86_64|aarch64) ;;
   460→        *) echo "Unsupported architecture: $ARCH" >&2; exit 1 ;;
   461→    esac
   462→    echo "  arch:        ${ARCH}"
   463→    echo ""
   464→
   465→    local DOCKER_VERSION="29.2.1"
   466→    local DOCKER_ROOTLESS_VERSION="29.2.1"
   467→    local SYSBOX_VERSION="0.6.7.10-tc"
   468→    local SYSBOX_TARBALL="sysbox-static-${ARCH}.tar.gz"
   469→
   470→    # SHA256 checksums — must match exactly; cache hits are also verified
   471→    # (protects against cache poisoning and mirror tampering)
   472→    local DOCKER_SHA256 DOCKER_ROOTLESS_SHA256 SYSBOX_SHA256
   473→    case "$ARCH" in
   474→        x86_64)
   475→            DOCKER_SHA256="995b1d0b51e96d551a3b49c552c0170bc6ce9f8b9e0866b8c15bbc67d1cf93a3"
   476→            DOCKER_ROOTLESS_SHA256="8c7b7783d8b391ca3183d9b5c7dea1794f6de69cfaa13c45f61fcd17d2b9c3ef"
   477→            SYSBOX_SHA256="9107dca08cc69c5871a0be7981dec3a3e8e5aa6e0924b7a6ca36df324357274b"
   478→            ;;
   479→        aarch64)
   480→            DOCKER_SHA256="236c5064473295320d4bf732fbbfc5b11b6b2dc446e8bc7ebb9222015fb36857"
   481→            DOCKER_ROOTLESS_SHA256="15895df8b46ff33179d357e61b600b5b51242f9b9587c0f66695689e62f57894"
   482→            SYSBOX_SHA256="6a543f863cf77cbec285f9eebbbe5d5e5c0f3fd3836347909b4ef1e4b3fc03ef"
   483→            ;;
   484→    esac
   485→
   486→    local DOCKER_URL="https://download.docker.com/linux/static/stable/${ARCH}/docker-${DOCKER_VERSION}.tgz"
   487→    local DOCKER_ROOTLESS_URL="https://download.docker.com/linux/static/stable/${ARCH}/docker-rootless-extras-${DOCKER_ROOTLESS_VERSION}.tgz"
   488→    local SYSBOX_URL="https://github.com/thieso2/sysbox/releases/download/v${SYSBOX_VERSION}/${SYSBOX_TARBALL}"
   489→
   490→    mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$RUN_DIR" "$ETC_DIR" "$BIN_DIR"
   491→    mkdir -p "$DOCKER_DATA" "$DOCKER_CONFIG_DIR"
   492→    mkdir -p "${RUN_DIR}/containerd"
   493→    mkdir -p "$CACHE_DIR"
   494→    mkdir -p "$SYSBOX_RUN_DIR"
   495→    mkdir -p "$SYSBOX_DATA_DIR"
   496→
   497→    # Create system user and group for this instance.
   498→    # dockerd runs as root but creates the socket owned by this group (--group flag),
   499→    # so operators simply join the group to get socket access without sudo.
   500→    if ! getent group "${INSTANCE_GROUP}" &>/dev/null; then

500→    if ! getent group "${INSTANCE_GROUP}" &>/dev/null; then
   501→        groupadd --system "${INSTANCE_GROUP}"
   502→        echo "  Created group ${INSTANCE_GROUP}"
   503→    else
   504→        echo "  Group ${INSTANCE_GROUP} already exists"
   505→    fi
   506→    if ! getent passwd "${INSTANCE_USER}" &>/dev/null; then
   507→        useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /bin/false \
   508→            --gid "${INSTANCE_GROUP}" "${INSTANCE_USER}"
   509→        echo "  Created user ${INSTANCE_USER}"
   510→    else
   511→        echo "  User ${INSTANCE_USER} already exists"
   512→    fi
   513→
   514→    # Allow sysbox-fs FUSE mounts at this instance's sysbox mountpoint.
   515→    # The default fusermount3 AppArmor profile (tightened in Ubuntu 25.10+)
   516→    # only permits FUSE mounts under $HOME, /mnt, /tmp, etc.  Without this
   517→    # override every sysbox container fails with a context-deadline-exceeded
   518→    # RPC error from sysbox-fs.
   519→    # Each instance appends a tagged block; destroy removes it.
   520→    if [ -d /etc/apparmor.d ]; then
   521→        mkdir -p /etc/apparmor.d/local
   522→        local apparmor_file="/etc/apparmor.d/local/fusermount3"
   523→        local apparmor_begin="# dockyard:${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}:begin"
   524→        local apparmor_end="# dockyard:${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}:end"
   525→        {
   526→            flock -x 9
   527→            if ! grep -qF "$apparmor_begin" "$apparmor_file" 2>/dev/null; then
   528→                {
   529→                    echo "$apparmor_begin"
   530→                    # Ubuntu 25.10+ comments out dac_override in the base fusermount3
   531→                    # profile (LP: #2122161). sysbox-fs needs it for FUSE mounts.
   532→                    echo "capability dac_override,"
   533→                    echo "mount fstype=fuse options=(nosuid,nodev) options in (ro,rw) -> ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR}/**/,"
   534→                    echo "umount ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR}/**/,"
   535→                    echo "$apparmor_end"
   536→                } >> "$apparmor_file"
   537→            fi
   538→        } 9>"${apparmor_file}.lock"
   539→        if [ -f /etc/apparmor.d/fusermount3 ]; then
   540→            apparmor_parser -r /etc/apparmor.d/fusermount3
   541→            echo "  AppArmor fusermount3 profile updated for ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR}"
   542→        fi
   543→    fi
   544→
   545→    verify_checksum() {
   546→        local file="$1" expected="$2" name="$3"
   547→        local actual
   548→        actual=$(sha256sum "$file" | awk '{print $1}')
   549→        if [ "$actual" != "$expected" ]; then
   550→            echo "Error: SHA256 mismatch for $name" >&2
   551→            echo "  expected: $expected" >&2
   552→            echo "  got:      $actual" >&2
   553→            rm -f "$file"
   554→            exit 1
   555→        fi
   556→    }
   557→
   558→    download() {
   559→        local url="$1"
   560→        local expected_sha256="$2"
   561→        local dest="${CACHE_DIR}/$(basename "$url")"
   562→        if [ -f "$dest" ]; then
   563→            echo "  cached: $(basename "$dest")"
   564→        else
   565→            echo "  downloading: $(basename "$url")"
   566→            curl -fsSL -o "${dest}.tmp" "$url" && mv "${dest}.tmp" "$dest"
   567→        fi
   568→        verify_checksum "$dest" "$expected_sha256" "$(basename "$url")"
   569→    }
   570→
   571→    echo "Downloading artifacts..."
   572→    download "$DOCKER_URL"          "$DOCKER_SHA256"
   573→    download "$DOCKER_ROOTLESS_URL" "$DOCKER_ROOTLESS_SHA256"
   574→    download "$SYSBOX_URL"          "$SYSBOX_SHA256"
   575→
   576→    # Use per-PID staging dirs for extraction so concurrent creates don't race
   577→    # on a shared extraction directory (all instances share the same CACHE_DIR).
   578→    local STAGING="${CACHE_DIR}/staging-$$"
   579→    mkdir -p "$STAGING"
   580→    trap 'rm -rf "$STAGING"' RETURN INT TERM
   581→
   582→    echo "Extracting Docker binaries..."
   583→    tar -xzf "${CACHE_DIR}/docker-${DOCKER_VERSION}.tgz" -C "$STAGING"
   584→    cp -f "${STAGING}/docker/"* "$BIN_DIR/"
   585→
   586→    echo "Extracting Docker rootless extras..."
   587→    tar -xzf "${CACHE_DIR}/docker-rootless-extras-${DOCKER_ROOTLESS_VERSION}.tgz" -C "$STAGING"
   588→    cp -f "${STAGING}/docker-rootless-extras/"* "$BIN_DIR/"
   589→
   590→    echo "Extracting sysbox static binaries..."
   591→    local SYSBOX_EXTRACT="${STAGING}/sysbox-static-${SYSBOX_VERSION}"
   592→    mkdir -p "$SYSBOX_EXTRACT"
   593→    tar -xzf "${CACHE_DIR}/${SYSBOX_TARBALL}" -C "$SYSBOX_EXTRACT"
   594→    # All three sysbox binaries go directly to BIN_DIR.
   595→    # sysbox-runc 0.6.7.9-tc parses --run-dir directly from os.Args in init(),
   596→    # bypassing urfave/cli v1 entirely. runtimeArgs in daemon.json now works.
   597→    # See: https://github.com/thieso2/sysbox/issues/5
   598→    for bin in sysbox-runc sysbox-mgr sysbox-fs; do
   599→        local src
   600→        src=$(find "$SYSBOX_EXTRACT" -name "$bin" -type f | head -1)
   601→        if [ -z "$src" ]; then
   602→            echo "Error: $bin not found in ${SYSBOX_TARBALL}" >&2
   603→            exit 1
   604→        fi
   605→        cp -f "$src" "$BIN_DIR/$bin"
   606→        chmod +x "$BIN_DIR/$bin"
   607→    done
   608→
   609→    chmod +x "$BIN_DIR"/*
   610→
   611→    # Rename docker CLI binary, replace with DOCKER_HOST wrapper
   612→    mv -f "${BIN_DIR}/docker" "${BIN_DIR}/docker-cli"
   613→    cat > "${BIN_DIR}/docker" <<DOCKEREOF
   614→#!/bin/bash
   615→export DOCKER_HOST="unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET}"
   616→export DOCKER_CONFIG="${DOCKER_CONFIG_DIR}"
   617→exec "\$(dirname "\$0")/docker-cli" "\$@"
   618→DOCKEREOF
   619→    chmod +x "${BIN_DIR}/docker"
   620→
   621→    echo "Installed binaries to ${BIN_DIR}/"
   622→
   623→    # Write daemon.json (embedded — no external file dependency)
   624→    # sysbox-runc 0.6.7.9-tc parses --run-dir from os.Args in init(), so
   625→    # runtimeArgs works correctly. No wrapper script needed.
   626→    cat > "${ETC_DIR}/daemon.json" <<DAEMONJSONEOF
   627→{
   628→  "default-runtime": "sysbox-runc",
   629→  "runtimes": {
   630→    "sysbox-runc": {
   631→      "path": "${BIN_DIR}/sysbox-runc",
   632→      "runtimeArgs": ["--run-dir", "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}"]
   633→    }
   634→  },
   635→  "storage-driver": "overlay2",
   636→  "userland-proxy-path": "${BIN_DIR}/docker-proxy",
   637→  "features": {
   638→    "buildkit": true
   639→  }
   640→}
   641→DAEMONJSONEOF
   642→    echo "Installed config to ${ETC_DIR}/daemon.json"
   643→
   644→    # Copy config file and dockyard.sh into instance.
   645→    # Installing as dockyard.sh means the script's own ../etc/dockyard.env
   646→    # auto-discovery works: ${BIN_DIR}/dockyard.sh finds ${ETC_DIR}/dockyard.env
   647→    # without needing DOCKYARD_ENV to be set.
   648→    cp "$LOADED_ENV_FILE" "${ETC_DIR}/dockyard.env"
   649→    cp "${SCRIPT_DIR}/dockyard.sh" "${BIN_DIR}/dockyard.sh"
   650→    chmod +x "${BIN_DIR}/dockyard.sh"
   651→    ln -sf dockyard.sh "${BIN_DIR}/dockyardctl"
   652→    echo "Installed env to ${ETC_DIR}/dockyard.env"
   653→    echo "Installed dockyard.sh to ${BIN_DIR}/dockyard.sh"
   654→
   655→    # Set ownership of the instance root so every file is attributed to the
   656→    # instance user/group. dockerd still runs as root, so it can write freely;
   657→    # the ownership is for identification and directory-level access control.
   658→    chown -R "${INSTANCE_USER}:${INSTANCE_GROUP}" "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}"
   659→    echo "Set ownership of ${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/ to ${INSTANCE_USER}:${INSTANCE_GROUP}"
   660→
   661→    # --- 2. Install systemd service ---
   662→    if [ "$INSTALL_SYSTEMD" = true ]; then
   663→        echo ""
   664→        cmd_enable
   665→    fi
   666→
   667→    # --- 3. Start daemon ---
   668→    if [ "$START_DAEMON" = true ]; then
   669→        echo ""
   670→        if [ "$INSTALL_SYSTEMD" = true ]; then
   671→            echo "Starting via systemd..."
   672→            systemctl start "${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
   673→            echo "  ${SERVICE_NAME}.service started"
   674→        else
   675→            echo "Starting directly..."
   676→            cmd_start
   677→        fi
   678→    fi
   679→
   680→    echo ""
   681→    echo "=== Installation complete ==="
   682→    echo ""
   683→    echo "To use:"
   684→    echo "  ${BIN_DIR}/docker run -ti alpine ash"
   685→    echo ""
   686→    echo "Manage this instance:"
   687→    echo "  ${BIN_DIR}/dockyard.sh status"
   688→    echo "  sudo ${BIN_DIR}/dockyard.sh verify"
   689→    echo "  sudo ${BIN_DIR}/dockyard.sh destroy"
   690→}
   691→
   692→cmd_enable() {
   693→    require_root
   694→
   695→    local SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
   696→
   697→    if [ -f "$SERVICE_FILE" ]; then
   698→        echo "Error: ${SERVICE_FILE} already exists." >&2
   699→        exit 1
   700→    fi
   701→
   702→    echo "Installing ${SERVICE_NAME}.service (with per-instance sysbox)..."
   703→
   704→    # Write the stack script (bakes all paths in at install time; no env file at runtime)
   705→    cat > "${BIN_DIR}/dockyard-stack" <<STACKEOF
   706→#!/bin/bash
   707→set -euo pipefail
   708→
   709→MGR_PID=""
   710→FS_PID=""
   711→CTR_PID=""
   712→DOCKERD_PID=""
   713→
   714→wait_for_socket() {
   715→    local sock="\$1" pid="\$2" name="\$3" i=0
   716→    while [ ! -S "\$sock" ]; do
   717→        sleep 1
   718→        i=\$((i+1))
   719→        if ! kill -0 "\$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   720→            echo "dockyard-stack: \$name exited unexpectedly" >&2
   721→            return 1
   722→        fi
   723→        if [ "\$i" -ge 30 ]; then
   724→            echo "dockyard-stack: \$name did not start within 30s" >&2
   725→            return 1
   726→        fi
   727→    done
   728→}
   729→
   730→cleanup() {
   731→    local code=\${1:-0}
   732→    for pid in "\$DOCKERD_PID" "\$CTR_PID" "\$FS_PID" "\$MGR_PID"; do
   733→        [ -z "\$pid" ] && continue
   734→        kill "\$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   735→        wait "\$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   736→    done
   737→    exit "\$code"
   738→}
   739→
   740→trap 'cleanup 0' TERM INT
   741→
   742→# --- Start sysbox-mgr ---
   743→${BIN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr --run-dir ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR} --data-root ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR} \
   744→    >>${LOG_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.log 2>&1 &
   745→MGR_PID=\$!
   746→echo "\$MGR_PID" > ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.pid
   747→wait_for_socket ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysmgr.sock "\$MGR_PID" sysbox-mgr || cleanup 1
   748→
   749→# --- Start sysbox-fs ---
   750→${BIN_DIR}/sysbox-fs --run-dir ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR} --mountpoint ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR} \
   751→    >>${LOG_DIR}/sysbox-fs.log 2>&1 &
   752→FS_PID=\$!
   753→echo "\$FS_PID" > ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-fs.pid
   754→wait_for_socket ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysfs.sock "\$FS_PID" sysbox-fs || cleanup 1
   755→
   756→# --- Start containerd ---
   757→${BIN_DIR}/containerd \
   758→    --root ${DOCKER_DATA}/containerd \
   759→    --state ${RUN_DIR}/containerd \
   760→    --address ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET} \
   761→    >>${LOG_DIR}/containerd.log 2>&1 &
   762→CTR_PID=\$!
   763→echo "\$CTR_PID" > ${RUN_DIR}/containerd.pid
   764→wait_for_socket ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET} "\$CTR_PID" containerd || cleanup 1
   765→
   766→# --- Start dockerd ---
   767→${BIN_DIR}/dockerd \
   768→    --config-file ${ETC_DIR}/daemon.json \
   769→    --containerd ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET} \
   770→    --data-root ${DOCKER_DATA} \
   771→    --exec-root ${RUN_DIR} \
   772→    --pidfile ${RUN_DIR}/dockerd.pid \
   773→    --bridge ${BRIDGE} \
   774→    --fixed-cidr ${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR} \
   775→    --default-address-pool base=${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE},size=${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE} \
   776→    --host unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET} \
   777→    --iptables=false \
   778→    --group ${INSTANCE_GROUP} \
   779→    >>${LOG_DIR}/dockerd.log 2>&1 &
   780→DOCKERD_PID=\$!
   781→wait_for_socket ${DOCKER_SOCKET} "\$DOCKERD_PID" dockerd || cleanup 1
   782→
   783→# Signal systemd that all daemon sockets are up.
   784→# || true: sd_notify returns non-zero when not running under systemd; don't abort.
   785→systemd-notify --ready 2>/dev/null || true
   786→
   787→# Monitor: if any daemon dies, trigger a restart
   788→while true; do
   789→    sleep 2 &
   790→    wait \$! 2>/dev/null || true
   791→    for check in "sysbox-mgr:\$MGR_PID" "sysbox-fs:\$FS_PID" "containerd:\$CTR_PID" "dockerd:\$DOCKERD_PID"; do
   792→        _name="\${check%%:*}"
   793→        _pid="\${check##*:}"
   794→        if [ -n "\$_pid" ] && ! kill -0 "\$_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   795→            echo "dockyard-stack: \$_name (pid \$_pid) died unexpectedly" >&2
   796→            cleanup 1
   797→        fi
   798→    done
   799→done
   800→STACKEOF
   801→    chmod 755 "${BIN_DIR}/dockyard-stack"
   802→    echo "  installed ${BIN_DIR}/dockyard-stack"
   803→
   804→    cat > "$SERVICE_FILE" <<SERVICEEOF
   805→[Unit]
   806→Description=Dockyard Docker (${SERVICE_NAME})
   807→After=network-online.target nss-lookup.target firewalld.service time-set.target
   808→Before=docker.service
   809→Wants=network-online.target
   810→StartLimitBurst=3
   811→StartLimitIntervalSec=60
   812→
   813→[Service]
   814→Type=notify
   815→NotifyAccess=all
   816→Environment=PATH=${BIN_DIR}:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
   817→
   818→# Create runtime and sysbox directories
   819→ExecStartPre=/bin/mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR} ${RUN_DIR}/containerd ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR} ${DOCKER_DATA}/containerd ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR}
   820→
   821→# Clean stale sockets (including sysbox — stale socket file fools the wait loop)
   822→ExecStartPre=-/bin/rm -f ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET} ${DOCKER_SOCKET}
   823→ExecStartPre=-/bin/rm -f ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysmgr.sock ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysfs.sock ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysfs-seccomp.sock
   824→
   825→# Enable IP forwarding
   826→ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -c 'sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null'
   827→
   828→# Create bridge
   829→ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -c 'if ! ip link show ${BRIDGE} &>/dev/null; then ip link add ${BRIDGE} type bridge && ip addr add ${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR} dev ${BRIDGE} && ip link set ${BRIDGE} up; fi'
   830→
   831→# Add iptables rules for container networking (bridge) — idempotent: -C check before -I
   832→ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -c 'iptables -C FORWARD -i ${BRIDGE} -o ${BRIDGE} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I FORWARD -i ${BRIDGE} -o ${BRIDGE} -j ACCEPT; iptables -C FORWARD -i ${BRIDGE} ! -o ${BRIDGE} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I FORWARD -i ${BRIDGE} ! -o ${BRIDGE} -j ACCEPT; iptables -C FORWARD -o ${BRIDGE} -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I FORWARD -o ${BRIDGE} -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT; iptables -t nat -C POSTROUTING -s ${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR} ! -o ${BRIDGE} -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null || iptables -t nat -I POSTROUTING -s ${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR} ! -o ${BRIDGE} -j MASQUERADE'
   833→
   834→# Add iptables rules for user-defined networks (from default-address-pool) — idempotent
   835→ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -c 'iptables -C FORWARD -s ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I FORWARD -s ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j ACCEPT; iptables -C FORWARD -d ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I FORWARD -d ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j ACCEPT; iptables -t nat -C POSTROUTING -s ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null || iptables -t nat -I POSTROUTING -s ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j MASQUERADE'
   836→
   837→# Stack script starts sysbox-mgr, sysbox-fs, containerd, dockerd; sends READY once sockets
   838→# are up; then monitors. ExecStartPost gates systemctl-start on full API readiness so callers
   839→# of "systemctl start" don't proceed until docker accepts connections.
   840→ExecStart=${BIN_DIR}/dockyard-stack
   841→
   842→# Wait until dockerd accepts API connections before systemctl start returns.
   843→ExecStartPost=/bin/bash -c 'i=0; while ! ${BIN_DIR}/docker-cli -H unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET} info >/dev/null 2>&1; do i=\$((i+1)); [ \$i -ge 360 ] && exit 1; sleep 0.5; done'
   844→
   845→# Clean up docker/containerd sockets
   846→ExecStopPost=-/bin/rm -f ${DOCKER_SOCKET} ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET}
   847→
   848→# Remove iptables rules (bridge)
   849→ExecStopPost=-/bin/bash -c 'iptables -D FORWARD -i ${BRIDGE} -o ${BRIDGE} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null; iptables -D FORWARD -i ${BRIDGE} ! -o ${BRIDGE} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null; iptables -D FORWARD -o ${BRIDGE} -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null; iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s ${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR} ! -o ${BRIDGE} -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null'
   850→
   851→# Remove iptables rules (user-defined networks)
   852→ExecStopPost=-/bin/bash -c 'iptables -D FORWARD -s ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null; iptables -D FORWARD -d ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null; iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s ${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE} -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null'
   853→
   854→# Remove bridge
   855→ExecStopPost=-/bin/bash -c 'if ip link show ${BRIDGE} &>/dev/null; then ip link set ${BRIDGE} down 2>/dev/null; ip link delete ${BRIDGE} 2>/dev/null; fi'
   856→
   857→# Clean up sysbox run dir
   858→ExecStopPost=-/bin/rm -rf ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}
   859→
   860→TimeoutStartSec=180
   861→TimeoutStopSec=30
   862→Restart=on-failure
   863→RestartSec=5
   864→
   865→LimitNPROC=infinity
   866→LimitCORE=infinity
   867→LimitNOFILE=infinity
   868→TasksMax=infinity
   869→Delegate=yes
   870→KillMode=process
   871→OOMScoreAdjust=-500
   872→
   873→[Install]
   874→WantedBy=multi-user.target
   875→SERVICEEOF
   876→    chmod 644 "$SERVICE_FILE"
   877→    systemctl daemon-reload
   878→    systemctl enable "${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
   879→    echo "  enabled ${SERVICE_NAME}.service (will start on boot)"
   880→    echo ""
   881→    echo "  sudo systemctl start ${SERVICE_NAME}    # start"
   882→    echo "  sudo systemctl status ${SERVICE_NAME}   # check status"
   883→    echo "  sudo journalctl -u ${SERVICE_NAME} -f   # follow logs"
   884→}
   885→
   886→cmd_disable() {
   887→    require_root
   888→
   889→    local SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
   890→
   891→    if [ -f "$SERVICE_FILE" ]; then
   892→        if systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE_NAME}.service"; then
   893→            echo "Stopping ${SERVICE_NAME}..."
   894→            systemctl stop "${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
   895→            echo "  stopped"
   896→        fi
   897→        if systemctl is-enabled --quiet "${SERVICE_NAME}.service" 2>/dev/null; then
   898→            systemctl disable "${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
   899→            echo "  disabled"
   900→        fi
   901→        rm -f "$SERVICE_FILE"
   902→        echo "Removed ${SERVICE_FILE}"
   903→    else
   904→        echo "Warning: ${SERVICE_FILE} does not exist." >&2
   905→    fi
   906→
   907→    systemctl daemon-reload
   908→}
   909→
   910→cmd_start() {
   911→    require_root
   912→
   913→    export PATH="${BIN_DIR}:${PATH}"
   914→
   915→    mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$RUN_DIR" "${RUN_DIR}/containerd" "$DOCKER_DATA/containerd"
   916→
   917→    # Clean up stale sockets/pids from previous runs
   918→    rm -f "$CONTAINERD_SOCKET" "$DOCKER_SOCKET"
   919→    rm -f "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysmgr.sock" "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysfs.sock" "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysfs-seccomp.sock"
   920→    for pidfile in "${RUN_DIR}/containerd.pid" "${RUN_DIR}/dockerd.pid"; do
   921→        if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
   922→            local pid
   923→            pid=$(cat "$pidfile")
   924→            kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null && sleep 1 || true
   925→            rm -f "$pidfile"
   926→        fi
   927→    done
   928→
   929→    # Cleanup helper: kill previously started daemons on failure
   930→    STARTED_PIDS=()
   931→    cleanup() {
   932→        echo "Startup failed — cleaning up..."
   933→        for pid in "${STARTED_PIDS[@]}"; do
   934→            kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   935→        done
   936→        if ip link show "$BRIDGE" &>/dev/null; then
   937→            ip link set "$BRIDGE" down 2>/dev/null || true
   938→            ip link delete "$BRIDGE" 2>/dev/null || true
   939→        fi
   940→        exit 1
   941→    }
   942→
   943→    # --- 1. Start per-instance sysbox daemons ---
   944→    mkdir -p "$SYSBOX_RUN_DIR" "$SYSBOX_DATA_DIR"
   945→
   946→    echo "Starting sysbox-mgr..."
   947→    "${BIN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr" --run-dir "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}" --data-root "${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR}" \
   948→        &>"${LOG_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.log" &
   949→    SYSBOX_MGR_PID=$!
   950→    echo "$SYSBOX_MGR_PID" > "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.pid"
   951→    STARTED_PIDS+=("$SYSBOX_MGR_PID")
   952→    wait_for_file "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysmgr.sock" "sysbox-mgr" 30 || cleanup
   953→    kill -0 "$SYSBOX_MGR_PID" 2>/dev/null || { echo "sysbox-mgr exited unexpectedly" >&2; cleanup; }
   954→    echo "  sysbox-mgr ready (pid ${SYSBOX_MGR_PID})"
   955→
   956→    echo "Starting sysbox-fs..."
   957→    "${BIN_DIR}/sysbox-fs" --run-dir "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}" --mountpoint "${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR}" \
   958→        &>"${LOG_DIR}/sysbox-fs.log" &
   959→    SYSBOX_FS_PID=$!
   960→    echo "$SYSBOX_FS_PID" > "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-fs.pid"
   961→    STARTED_PIDS+=("$SYSBOX_FS_PID")
   962→    wait_for_file "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysfs.sock" "sysbox-fs" 30 || cleanup
   963→    kill -0 "$SYSBOX_FS_PID" 2>/dev/null || { echo "sysbox-fs exited unexpectedly" >&2; cleanup; }
   964→    echo "  sysbox-fs ready (pid ${SYSBOX_FS_PID})"
   965→
   966→    # --- 2. Create bridge ---
   967→    if ! ip link show "$BRIDGE" &>/dev/null; then
   968→        echo "Creating bridge ${BRIDGE}..."
   969→        ip link add "$BRIDGE" type bridge
   970→        ip addr add "$DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR" dev "$BRIDGE"
   971→        ip link set "$BRIDGE" up
   972→    else
   973→        echo "Bridge ${BRIDGE} already exists"
   974→    fi
   975→
   976→    # Enable IP forwarding
   977→    sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 >/dev/null
   978→
   979→    # Bridge rules (idempotent)
   980→    iptables -C FORWARD -i "$BRIDGE" -o "$BRIDGE" -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null ||
   981→        iptables -I FORWARD -i "$BRIDGE" -o "$BRIDGE" -j ACCEPT
   982→    iptables -C FORWARD -i "$BRIDGE" ! -o "$BRIDGE" -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null ||
   983→        iptables -I FORWARD -i "$BRIDGE" ! -o "$BRIDGE" -j ACCEPT
   984→    iptables -C FORWARD -o "$BRIDGE" -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null ||
   985→        iptables -I FORWARD -o "$BRIDGE" -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
   986→    iptables -t nat -C POSTROUTING -s "$DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR" ! -o "$BRIDGE" -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null ||
   987→        iptables -t nat -I POSTROUTING -s "$DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR" ! -o "$BRIDGE" -j MASQUERADE
   988→
   989→    # Pool rules
   990→    iptables -C FORWARD -s "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null ||
   991→        iptables -I FORWARD -s "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j ACCEPT
   992→    iptables -C FORWARD -d "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null ||
   993→        iptables -I FORWARD -d "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j ACCEPT
   994→    iptables -t nat -C POSTROUTING -s "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null ||
   995→        iptables -t nat -I POSTROUTING -s "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j MASQUERADE
   996→
   997→    # --- 3. Start containerd ---
   998→    echo "Starting containerd..."
   999→    "${BIN_DIR}/containerd" \

1000→        --root "$DOCKER_DATA/containerd" \
  1001→        --state "${RUN_DIR}/containerd" \
  1002→        --address "$CONTAINERD_SOCKET" \
  1003→        &>"${LOG_DIR}/containerd.log" &
  1004→    CONTAINERD_PID=$!
  1005→    echo "$CONTAINERD_PID" > "${RUN_DIR}/containerd.pid"
  1006→    STARTED_PIDS+=("$CONTAINERD_PID")
  1007→
  1008→    wait_for_file "$CONTAINERD_SOCKET" "containerd" || cleanup
  1009→    echo "  containerd ready (pid ${CONTAINERD_PID})"
  1010→
  1011→    # --- 4. Start dockerd ---
  1012→    echo "Starting dockerd..."
  1013→    "${BIN_DIR}/dockerd" \
  1014→        --config-file "${ETC_DIR}/daemon.json" \
  1015→        --containerd "$CONTAINERD_SOCKET" \
  1016→        --data-root "$DOCKER_DATA" \
  1017→        --exec-root "$RUN_DIR" \
  1018→        --pidfile "${RUN_DIR}/dockerd.pid" \
  1019→        --bridge "$BRIDGE" \
  1020→        --fixed-cidr "$DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR" \
  1021→        --default-address-pool "base=${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE},size=${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE}" \
  1022→        --host "unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET}" \
  1023→        --iptables=false \
  1024→        --group "${INSTANCE_GROUP}" \
  1025→        &>"${LOG_DIR}/dockerd.log" &
  1026→    DOCKERD_PID=$!
  1027→    STARTED_PIDS+=("$DOCKERD_PID")
  1028→
  1029→    wait_for_file "$DOCKER_SOCKET" "dockerd" 30 || cleanup
  1030→    echo "  dockerd ready (pid ${DOCKERD_PID})"
  1031→
  1032→    echo "=== All daemons started ==="
  1033→    echo "Run: DOCKER_HOST=unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET} docker ps"
  1034→}
  1035→
  1036→cmd_stop() {
  1037→    require_root
  1038→
  1039→    # Reverse startup order: dockerd -> containerd -> sysbox
  1040→    stop_daemon dockerd "${RUN_DIR}/dockerd.pid" 20
  1041→    stop_daemon containerd "${RUN_DIR}/containerd.pid" 10
  1042→    stop_daemon sysbox-fs "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-fs.pid" 10
  1043→    stop_daemon sysbox-mgr "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.pid" 10
  1044→    rm -rf "$SYSBOX_RUN_DIR"
  1045→
  1046→    # Clean up sockets
  1047→    rm -f "$DOCKER_SOCKET" "$CONTAINERD_SOCKET"
  1048→
  1049→    # Remove iptables rules (bridge)
  1050→    iptables -D FORWARD -i "$BRIDGE" -o "$BRIDGE" -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || true
  1051→    iptables -D FORWARD -i "$BRIDGE" ! -o "$BRIDGE" -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || true
  1052→    iptables -D FORWARD -o "$BRIDGE" -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || true
  1053→    iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s "$DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR" ! -o "$BRIDGE" -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null || true
  1054→    # Remove iptables rules (pool)
  1055→    iptables -D FORWARD -s "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || true
  1056→    iptables -D FORWARD -d "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || true
  1057→    iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s "$DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE" -j MASQUERADE 2>/dev/null || true
  1058→
  1059→    # Remove bridge
  1060→    if ip link show "$BRIDGE" &>/dev/null; then
  1061→        ip link set "$BRIDGE" down
  1062→        ip link delete "$BRIDGE"
  1063→        echo "Bridge ${BRIDGE} removed"
  1064→    fi
  1065→
  1066→    # Remove leftover user-defined network bridges from the pool range
  1067→    cleanup_pool_bridges
  1068→
  1069→    echo "=== All daemons stopped ==="
  1070→}
  1071→
  1072→cmd_status() {
  1073→    echo "=== Dockyard Docker Status ==="
  1074→    echo ""
  1075→
  1076→    echo "Variables:"
  1077→    echo "  DOCKYARD_ROOT=${DOCKYARD_ROOT}"
  1078→    echo "  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}"
  1079→    echo "  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR=${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR}"
  1080→    echo "  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR}"
  1081→    echo "  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE}"
  1082→    echo "  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE=${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE}"
  1083→    echo ""
  1084→
  1085→    echo "Derived:"
  1086→    echo "  RUN_DIR=${RUN_DIR}"
  1087→    echo "  BRIDGE=${BRIDGE}"
  1088→    echo "  SERVICE_NAME=${SERVICE_NAME}"
  1089→    echo "  DOCKER_SOCKET=${DOCKER_SOCKET}"
  1090→    echo "  CONTAINERD_SOCKET=${CONTAINERD_SOCKET}"
  1091→    echo ""
  1092→
  1093→    # --- systemd services ---
  1094→    local SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
  1095→
  1096→    if [ -f "$SERVICE_FILE" ]; then
  1097→        echo "systemd (docker): $(systemctl is-active "${SERVICE_NAME}.service" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown") ($(systemctl is-enabled "${SERVICE_NAME}.service" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown"))"
  1098→    else
  1099→        echo "systemd (docker): not installed"
  1100→    fi
  1101→
  1102→    # --- pid checks ---
  1103→    check_pid() {
  1104→        local name="$1"
  1105→        local pidfile="$2"
  1106→        if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
  1107→            local pid
  1108→            pid=$(cat "$pidfile")
  1109→            if [ -d "/proc/${pid}" ]; then
  1110→                echo "${name}: running (pid ${pid})"
  1111→            else
  1112→                echo "${name}: dead (stale pid ${pid})"
  1113→            fi
  1114→        else
  1115→            echo "${name}: not running"
  1116→        fi
  1117→    }
  1118→
  1119→    check_pid "sysbox-mgr" "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.pid"
  1120→    check_pid "sysbox-fs " "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-fs.pid"
  1121→    check_pid "containerd" "${RUN_DIR}/containerd.pid"
  1122→    check_pid "dockerd   " "${RUN_DIR}/dockerd.pid"
  1123→
  1124→    # --- bridge ---
  1125→    if ip link show "$BRIDGE" &>/dev/null; then
  1126→        local local_ip
  1127→        local_ip=$(ip -4 addr show "$BRIDGE" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'inet \K[^ ]+' || echo "no ip")
  1128→        echo "bridge:     ${BRIDGE} (${local_ip})"
  1129→    else
  1130→        echo "bridge:     ${BRIDGE} not found"
  1131→    fi
  1132→
  1133→    # --- sockets ---
  1134→    if [ -e "$DOCKER_SOCKET" ]; then
  1135→        echo "socket:     ${DOCKER_SOCKET}"
  1136→    else
  1137→        echo "socket:     ${DOCKER_SOCKET} not found"
  1138→    fi
  1139→
  1140→    if [ -e "$CONTAINERD_SOCKET" ]; then
  1141→        echo "containerd: ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET}"
  1142→    else
  1143→        echo "containerd: ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET} not found"
  1144→    fi
  1145→
  1146→    # --- connectivity test ---
  1147→    echo ""
  1148→    echo "Connectivity:"
  1149→    if [ -e "$DOCKER_SOCKET" ]; then
  1150→        echo "  DOCKER_HOST=unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET} docker run --rm alpine /bin/ash -c 'ping -c 3 heise.de'"
  1151→        DOCKER_HOST="unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET}" docker run --rm alpine /bin/ash -c 'ping -c 3 heise.de' 2>&1 | sed 's/^/  /'
  1152→    else
  1153→        echo "  skipped (docker socket not found)"
  1154→    fi
  1155→
  1156→    # --- paths ---
  1157→    echo ""
  1158→    echo "Paths:"
  1159→    echo "  root:     ${DOCKYARD_ROOT}"
  1160→    echo "  data:     ${DOCKER_DATA}"
  1161→    echo "  logs:     ${LOG_DIR}"
  1162→}
  1163→
  1164→cmd_destroy() {
  1165→    local YES=false
  1166→    local KEEP_DATA=false
  1167→    for arg in "$@"; do
  1168→        case "$arg" in
  1169→            --yes|-y)       YES=true ;;
  1170→            --keep-data|-k) KEEP_DATA=true ;;
  1171→            -h|--help)      usage ;;
  1172→        esac
  1173→    done
  1174→
  1175→    require_root
  1176→
  1177→    local SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
  1178→
  1179→    if [[ "$KEEP_DATA" == true ]]; then
  1180→        echo "This will remove the dockyard instance (binaries and config only — data preserved):"
  1181→        echo "  ${SERVICE_FILE}"
  1182→        echo "  ${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/  (except ${DOCKER_DATA}/)"
  1183→    else
  1184→        echo "This will remove all installed dockyard docker files:"
  1185→        echo "  ${SERVICE_FILE}    (docker systemd service)"
  1186→        echo "  ${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/  (all instance data: binaries, config, data, logs, sockets)"
  1187→        local data_size
  1188→        data_size=$(du -sh "${DOCKER_DATA}" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1 || echo "unknown")
  1189→        echo ""
  1190→        echo "Warning: this will permanently delete container data:"
  1191→        echo "  ${DOCKER_DATA}/  (~${data_size})"
  1192→        echo "  Use --keep-data (-k) to preserve container data."
  1193→    fi
  1194→    echo ""
  1195→    if [[ "$YES" != true ]]; then
  1196→        read -p "Continue? [y/N] " confirm
  1197→        if [[ ! "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
  1198→            echo "Aborted."
  1199→            exit 0
  1200→        fi
  1201→    fi
  1202→
  1203→    # --- 1. Stop and remove systemd service (or stop daemons directly) ---
  1204→    if [ -f "$SERVICE_FILE" ]; then
  1205→        cmd_disable
  1206→    else
  1207→        # No systemd service — stop daemons directly
  1208→        for pidfile in "${RUN_DIR}/dockerd.pid" "${RUN_DIR}/containerd.pid"; do
  1209→            if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
  1210→                local pid
  1211→                pid=$(cat "$pidfile")
  1212→                if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
  1213→                    echo "Stopping pid ${pid}..."
  1214→                    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
  1215→                fi
  1216→                rm -f "$pidfile"
  1217→            fi
  1218→        done
  1219→        stop_daemon sysbox-fs "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-fs.pid" 10
  1220→        stop_daemon sysbox-mgr "${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.pid" 10
  1221→        rm -rf "$SYSBOX_RUN_DIR"
  1222→        rm -f "$DOCKER_SOCKET" "$CONTAINERD_SOCKET"
  1223→        if ip link show "$BRIDGE" &>/dev/null; then
  1224→            ip link set "$BRIDGE" down 2>/dev/null || true
  1225→            ip link delete "$BRIDGE" 2>/dev/null || true
  1226→        fi
  1227→        sleep 2
  1228→    fi
  1229→
  1230→    # --- 1.5. Remove leftover user-defined network bridges from the pool ---
  1231→    cleanup_pool_bridges
  1232→
  1233→    # --- 2. Remove AppArmor fusermount3 entry for this instance ---
  1234→    local apparmor_file="/etc/apparmor.d/local/fusermount3"
  1235→    local apparmor_begin="# dockyard:${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}:begin"
  1236→    if grep -qF "$apparmor_begin" "$apparmor_file" 2>/dev/null; then
  1237→        {
  1238→            flock -x 9
  1239→            awk -v start="$apparmor_begin" \
  1240→                -v stop="# dockyard:${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}:end" \
  1241→                '$0 == start { skip=1 } skip { if ($0 == stop) { skip=0 }; next } { print }' \
  1242→                "$apparmor_file" > "${apparmor_file}.tmp" \
  1243→                && mv "${apparmor_file}.tmp" "$apparmor_file"
  1244→        } 9>"${apparmor_file}.lock"
  1245→        if [ -f /etc/apparmor.d/fusermount3 ]; then
  1246→            apparmor_parser -r /etc/apparmor.d/fusermount3 2>/dev/null || true
  1247→        fi
  1248→        echo "Removed AppArmor fusermount3 entry for ${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}"
  1249→    fi
  1250→
  1251→    # --- 3. Remove instance root (selective or full) ---
  1252→    if [ -d "$DOCKYARD_ROOT" ]; then
  1253→        if [[ "$KEEP_DATA" == true ]]; then
  1254→            rm -rf "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/bin"
  1255→            rm -rf "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/etc"
  1256→            rm -rf "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/log"
  1257→            rm -rf "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/run"
  1258→            rm -rf "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/lib/sysbox"
  1259→            rm -rf "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/lib/docker-config"
  1260→            echo "Removed instance files from ${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/"
  1261→            echo "Data preserved at ${DOCKER_DATA}"
  1262→        else
  1263→            rm -rf "$DOCKYARD_ROOT"
  1264→            echo "Removed ${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/"
  1265→        fi
  1266→    fi
  1267→
  1268→    # --- 4. Remove instance user and group ---
  1269→    if getent passwd "${INSTANCE_USER}" &>/dev/null; then
  1270→        userdel "${INSTANCE_USER}" 2>/dev/null || true
  1271→        echo "Removed user ${INSTANCE_USER}"
  1272→    fi
  1273→    if getent group "${INSTANCE_GROUP}" &>/dev/null; then
  1274→        groupdel "${INSTANCE_GROUP}" 2>/dev/null || true
  1275→        echo "Removed group ${INSTANCE_GROUP}"
  1276→    fi
  1277→
  1278→    echo ""
  1279→    echo "=== Uninstall complete ==="
  1280→}
  1281→
  1282→# ── Verify ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1283→# Smoke-tests a running dockyard instance: service, socket, API, containers,
  1284→# outbound networking, and Docker-in-Docker (sysbox).
  1285→# Exits 0 only when every check passes.
  1286→
  1287→cmd_verify() {
  1288→    local _p=0 _f=0
  1289→    local _d="${BIN_DIR}/docker"
  1290→    local _s="unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET}"
  1291→    local out
  1292→
  1293→    _pass() { echo "  PASS: $1"; _p=$((_p + 1)); }
  1294→    _fail() { echo "  FAIL: $1 — $2" >&2; _f=$((_f + 1)); }
  1295→
  1296→    echo "=== dockyard verify: ${SERVICE_NAME} ==="
  1297→    echo ""
  1298→
  1299→    # 1. systemd service active
  1300→    if systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE_NAME}"; then
  1301→        _pass "systemd service ${SERVICE_NAME} active"
  1302→    else
  1303→        _fail "systemd service" "${SERVICE_NAME} is $(systemctl is-active "${SERVICE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
  1304→    fi
  1305→
  1306→    # 2. docker socket exists
  1307→    if [ -S "${DOCKER_SOCKET}" ]; then
  1308→        _pass "docker socket exists"
  1309→    else
  1310→        _fail "docker socket" "${DOCKER_SOCKET} not found"
  1311→    fi
  1312→
  1313→    # 3. docker info (API reachable)
  1314→    if DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  1315→        _pass "docker info (API reachable)"
  1316→    else
  1317→        out=$(DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" info 2>&1 | tail -2)
  1318→        _fail "docker info" "$out"
  1319→    fi
  1320→
  1321→    # 4. basic container run
  1322→    out=$(DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" run --rm alpine echo verify-ok 2>&1)
  1323→    if echo "$out" | grep -q "verify-ok"; then
  1324→        _pass "container run (alpine echo)"
  1325→    else
  1326→        _fail "container run" "$out"
  1327→    fi
  1328→
  1329→    # 5. outbound networking
  1330→    if DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" run --rm alpine ping -c3 -W2 1.1.1.1 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  1331→        _pass "outbound networking (ping 1.1.1.1)"
  1332→    else
  1333→        _fail "outbound networking" "ping 1.1.1.1 failed from container"
  1334→    fi
  1335→
  1336→    # 6. Docker-in-Docker via sysbox
  1337→    local cname="dockyard-verify-$$"
  1338→    DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" rm -f "$cname" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  1339→    if DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" run -d --name "$cname" docker:26.1-dind >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  1340→        local ready=false i
  1341→        for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  1342→            if DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" exec "$cname" docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  1343→                ready=true
  1344→                break
  1345→            fi
  1346→            sleep 2
  1347→        done
  1348→        if $ready; then
  1349→            # Preload alpine from host cache to avoid Docker Hub rate limits.
  1350→            if [ -f /var/tmp/alpine.tar ]; then
  1351→                DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" exec -i "$cname" docker load < /var/tmp/alpine.tar >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  1352→            fi
  1353→            out=$(DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" exec "$cname" docker run --rm alpine echo dind-ok 2>&1)
  1354→            if echo "$out" | grep -q "dind-ok"; then
  1355→                _pass "Docker-in-Docker (inner container via sysbox)"
  1356→            else
  1357→                _fail "DinD inner container" "$out"
  1358→            fi
  1359→        else
  1360→            _fail "DinD" "inner dockerd not ready after 60s"
  1361→        fi
  1362→        DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" rm -f "$cname" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  1363→    else
  1364→        out=$(DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" run --name "$cname" docker:26.1-dind 2>&1 | head -3)
  1365→        DOCKER_HOST="$_s" "$_d" rm -f "$cname" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  1366→        _fail "DinD" "could not start docker:26.1-dind — $out"
  1367→    fi
  1368→
  1369→    echo ""
  1370→    if [ "$_f" -eq 0 ]; then
  1371→        echo "All ${_p} checks passed."
  1372→    else
  1373→        echo "${_p} passed, ${_f} failed."
  1374→    fi
  1375→    return "$_f"
  1376→}
  1377→
  1378→# ── Usage ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1379→
  1380→usage() {
  1381→    cat <<'EOF'
  1382→Usage: ./dockyard.sh <command> [options]
  1383→
  1384→Commands:
  1385→  gen-env     Generate a conflict-free dockyard.env config file
  1386→  create      Download binaries, install config, set up systemd, start daemon
  1387→  enable      Install systemd service for this instance
  1388→  disable     Remove systemd service for this instance
  1389→  start       Start daemons manually (without systemd)
  1390→  stop        Stop manually started daemons
  1391→  status      Show instance status
  1392→  verify      Smoke-test a running instance (service, containers, DinD, networking)
  1393→  destroy     Stop and remove everything
  1394→
  1395→All commands except gen-env require a config file:
  1396→  1. $DOCKYARD_ENV (if set)
  1397→  2. ./dockyard.env (in current directory)
  1398→  3. ../etc/dockyard.env (relative to script — for installed copy)
  1399→  4. $DOCKYARD_ROOT/etc/dockyard.env
  1400→
  1401→Examples:
  1402→  ./dockyard.sh gen-env
  1403→  sudo ./dockyard.sh create
  1404→  sudo ./dockyard.sh create --no-systemd --no-start
  1405→  sudo ./dockyard.sh start
  1406→  sudo ./dockyard.sh stop
  1407→  ./dockyard.sh status
  1408→  sudo ./dockyard.sh verify
  1409→  sudo ./dockyard.sh destroy
  1410→  sudo ./dockyard.sh destroy --keep-data   # preserve container data
  1411→
  1412→  # Multiple instances
  1413→  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=test_ DOCKYARD_ROOT=/test ./dockyard.sh gen-env
  1414→  DOCKYARD_ENV=./dockyard.env sudo -E ./dockyard.sh create
  1415→EOF
  1416→    exit 0
  1417→}
  1418→
  1419→gen_env_usage() {
  1420→    cat <<'EOF'
  1421→Usage: ./dockyard.sh gen-env [OPTIONS]
  1422→
  1423→Generate a dockyard.env config file with randomized, conflict-free networks.
  1424→
  1425→Options:
  1426→  --nocheck     Skip all conflict checks
  1427→  -h, --help    Show this help
  1428→
  1429→Output: ./dockyard.env (or $DOCKYARD_ENV if set). Errors if file exists.
  1430→
  1431→Override any variable via environment:
  1432→  DOCKYARD_ROOT           Installation root (default: /dockyard)
  1433→  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX  Prefix for bridge/service (default: dy_)
  1434→  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR    Bridge IP/mask (default: random from 172.16.0.0/12)
  1435→  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR     Container subnet (default: derived from bridge)
  1436→  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE      Address pool base (default: random from 172.16.0.0/12)
  1437→  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE      Pool subnet size (default: 24)
  1438→
  1439→Examples:
  1440→  ./dockyard.sh gen-env
  1441→  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=test_ ./dockyard.sh gen-env
  1442→  DOCKYARD_ROOT=/docker2 DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=d2_ ./dockyard.sh gen-env
  1443→  ./dockyard.sh gen-env --nocheck
  1444→EOF
  1445→    exit 0
  1446→}
  1447→
  1448→create_usage() {
  1449→    cat <<'EOF'
  1450→Usage: sudo ./dockyard.sh create [OPTIONS]
  1451→
  1452→Create a dockyard instance: download binaries, install config,
  1453→set up systemd service, and start the daemon.
  1454→
  1455→Requires a dockyard.env config file (run gen-env first).
  1456→
  1457→Options:
  1458→  --no-systemd    Skip systemd service installation
  1459→  --no-start      Don't start the daemon after install
  1460→  -h, --help      Show this help
  1461→
  1462→Examples:
  1463→  ./dockyard.sh gen-env && sudo ./dockyard.sh create
  1464→  sudo ./dockyard.sh create --no-systemd --no-start
  1465→  DOCKYARD_ENV=./custom.env sudo -E ./dockyard.sh create
  1466→EOF
  1467→    exit 0
  1468→}
  1469→
  1470→# ── Dispatch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1471→
  1472→COMMAND="${1:-}"
  1473→shift || true
  1474→
  1475→case "$COMMAND" in
  1476→    gen-env)
  1477→        cmd_gen_env "$@"
  1478→        ;;
  1479→    create)
  1480→        if ! try_load_env; then
  1481→            echo "No config found — auto-generating with random networks..."
  1482→            cmd_gen_env
  1483→            load_env
  1484→        fi
  1485→        derive_vars
  1486→        cmd_create "$@"
  1487→        ;;
  1488→    enable)
  1489→        load_env
  1490→        derive_vars
  1491→        cmd_enable
  1492→        ;;
  1493→    disable)
  1494→        load_env
  1495→        derive_vars
  1496→        cmd_disable
  1497→        ;;
  1498→    start)
  1499→        load_env
  1500→        derive_vars
  1501→        cmd_start
  1502→        ;;
  1503→    stop)
  1504→        load_env
  1505→        derive_vars
  1506→        cmd_stop
  1507→        ;;
  1508→    status)
  1509→        load_env
  1510→        derive_vars
  1511→        cmd_status
  1512→        ;;
  1513→    verify)
  1514→        load_env
  1515→        derive_vars
  1516→        cmd_verify
  1517→        ;;
  1518→    destroy)
  1519→        load_env
  1520→        derive_vars
  1521→        cmd_destroy "$@"
  1522→        ;;
  1523→    -h|--help|"")
  1524→        usage
  1525→        ;;
  1526→    *)
  1527→        echo "Unknown command: $COMMAND" >&2
  1528→        usage
  1529→        ;;
  1530→esac

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Sandcastle installer
     3→# Usage: installer.sh [gen-env|install|reset|uninstall|help]
     4→set -euo pipefail
     5→
     6→# ═══ Colors & helpers ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     7→
     8→RED='\033[0;31m'
     9→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    10→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    11→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    12→NC='\033[0m'
    13→
    14→info()  { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    15→ok()    { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $*"; }
    16→warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    17→error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*" >&2; }
    18→die()   { error "$@"; exit 1; }
    19→
    20→WRITTEN_FILES=()
    21→wrote() { WRITTEN_FILES+=("$1"); }
    22→print_written_files() {
    23→  if [ ${#WRITTEN_FILES[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    24→    echo ""
    25→    info "Files created/updated:"
    26→    for f in "${WRITTEN_FILES[@]}"; do
    27→      echo -e "    ${f}"
    28→    done
    29→  fi
    30→  WRITTEN_FILES=()
    31→}
    32→
    33→require_root() {
    34→  [ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ] || die "This command must be run as root (use sudo)"
    35→}
    36→
    37→# ═══ Parse command ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    38→
    39→COMMAND="${1:-install}"
    40→shift 2>/dev/null || true
    41→
    42→case "$COMMAND" in
    43→  gen-env|install|update|reset|uninstall) ;;
    44→  help|-h|--help) COMMAND="help" ;;
    45→  *) die "Unknown command: $COMMAND (use 'help' for usage)" ;;
    46→esac
    47→
    48→# ═══ Help ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    49→
    50→if [ "$COMMAND" = "help" ]; then
    51→  cat <<'USAGE'
    52→Usage: installer.sh [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
    53→
    54→Sandcastle installer — sets up Docker (via Dockyard), Traefik, and the
    55→Sandcastle platform.
    56→
    57→Commands:
    58→  gen-env      Generate sandcastle.env config file (default: ./sandcastle.env)
    59→  install      Install or upgrade Sandcastle (default)
    60→  update       Pull latest images and restart services
    61→  reset        Tear down existing install, then reinstall
    62→  uninstall    Remove Sandcastle completely
    63→  help         Show this help message
    64→
    65→install options:
    66→  --from-backup <file>   Restore data from a backup file after installing.
    67→                         Secrets, database, and user data are loaded from the
    68→                         backup instead of being generated fresh.
    69→                         SANDCASTLE_HOST is still required in sandcastle.env.
    70→                         SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD are not required.
    71→
    72→Environment:
    73→  SANDCASTLE_ENV   Path to config file (default search order:
    74→                   ./sandcastle.env → <script_dir>/sandcastle.env →
    75→                   $SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env)
    76→
    77→Workflow (fresh install):
    78→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config
    79→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # edit to taste
    80→  3. sudo installer.sh install         # install (finds ./sandcastle.env)
    81→
    82→Workflow (restore from backup):
    83→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config (set SANDCASTLE_HOST)
    84→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # set host, TLS mode — no admin password needed
    85→  3. sudo installer.sh install --from-backup ./sandcastle-backup-*.tar.zst
    86→USAGE
    87→  exit 0
    88→fi
    89→
    90→# ═══ load_env ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    91→
    92→load_env() {
    93→  local script_dir
    94→  script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    95→  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
    96→
    97→  LOADED_ENV_FILE=""
    98→  if [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-}" ]; then
    99→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ENV" ] && die "Env file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ENV"
   100→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")" && pwd)/$(basename "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")"
   101→  elif [ -f "./sandcastle.env" ]; then
   102→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/sandcastle.env"
   103→  elif [ -f "$script_dir/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   104→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$script_dir/sandcastle.env"
   105→  elif [ -f "$home/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   106→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$home/etc/sandcastle.env"
   107→  fi
   108→
   109→  if [ -n "$LOADED_ENV_FILE" ]; then
   110→    set -a
   111→    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   112→    source "$LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   113→    set +a
   114→    info "Loaded $LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   115→  fi
   116→}
   117→
   118→# ═══ derive_vars ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   119→
   120→derive_vars() {
   121→  SANDCASTLE_HOME="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
   122→  APP_IMAGE="${APP_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest}"
   123→  SANDBOX_IMAGE="${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest}"
   124→  SANDCASTLE_USER="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandcastle}"
   125→  SANDCASTLE_GROUP="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP:-$SANDCASTLE_USER}"
   126→  SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
   127→  SANDCASTLE_GID="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"
   128→  SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}"
   129→  SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
   130→  TCP_PORT_MIN="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN:-3000}"
   131→  TCP_PORT_MAX="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX:-3099}"
   132→  SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"
   133→  SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER:-admin}"
   134→
   135→  DOCKYARD_ROOT="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-$SANDCASTLE_HOME}"
   136→  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-sc_}"
   137→
   138→  # Single RFC 1918 /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
   139→  # Must be a private range (10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x).
   140→  SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET:-10.89.0.0/16}"
   141→  _priv_base="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET%%/*}"        # e.g. "10.89.0.0"
   142→  _priv_prefix="${_priv_base%.*.*}"                  # e.g. "10.89"
   143→  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.1/24}"
   144→  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.0/24}"
   145→  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET}}"
   146→  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"
   147→
   148→  DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker.sock"
   149→  DOCKER="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker-runtime/bin/docker"
   150→
   151→  ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture 2>/dev/null || echo "amd64")
   152→
   153→  # Resolve password from file if needed
   154→  if [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE:-}" ]; then
   155→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE" ] && die "Password file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE"
   156→    SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD="$(cat "$SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE")"
   157→  fi
   158→}
   159→
   160→# ═══ setup_ssh_keys ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   161→# Copy deploying user's SSH keys to sandcastle user
   162→
   163→setup_ssh_keys() {
   164→  local deploying_user="${SUDO_USER:-}"
   165→
   166→  if [ -z "$deploying_user" ]; then
   167→    warn "No SUDO_USER found — skipping SSH key setup"
   168→    return
   169→  fi
   170→
   171→  local source_keys="/home/${deploying_user}/.ssh/authorized_keys"
   172→  local target_dir="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/.ssh"
   173→  local target_keys="${target_dir}/authorized_keys"
   174→
   175→  if [ ! -f "$source_keys" ]; then
   176→    warn "No SSH keys found for user '${deploying_user}' — skipping SSH key setup"
   177→    return
   178→  fi
   179→
   180→  info "Setting up SSH keys for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'..."
   181→
   182→  # Create .ssh directory if it doesn't exist
   183→  mkdir -p "$target_dir"
   184→  chmod 700 "$target_dir"
   185→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$target_dir"
   186→
   187→  # Copy authorized_keys
   188→  cp "$source_keys" "$target_keys"
   189→  chmod 600 "$target_keys"
   190→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$target_keys"
   191→
   192→  ok "SSH keys configured for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'"
   193→}
   194→
   195→# ═══ setup_passwordless_sudo ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   196→# Add sandcastle user to sudoers with NOPASSWD:ALL
   197→
   198→setup_passwordless_sudo() {
   199→  local sudoers_file="/etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle"
   200→
   201→  info "Configuring passwordless sudo for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'..."
   202→
   203→  echo "${SANDCASTLE_USER} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" > "$sudoers_file"
   204→  chmod 440 "$sudoers_file"
   205→
   206→  # Validate sudoers syntax
   207→  if ! visudo -cf "$sudoers_file" &>/dev/null; then
   208→    rm -f "$sudoers_file"
   209→    warn "Failed to validate sudoers file — skipping passwordless sudo setup"
   210→    return
   211→  fi
   212→
   213→  wrote "$sudoers_file"
   214→  ok "Passwordless sudo configured for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'"
   215→}
   216→
   217→# ═══ setup_bashrc_path ════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   218→# Add docker-runtime/bin to PATH in .profile and .bashrc (idempotent)
   219→
   220→setup_bashrc_path() {
   221→  local profile="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/.profile"
   222→  local bashrc="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/.bashrc"
   223→  local path_export="export PATH=${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/docker-runtime/bin:${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/bin:\$PATH"
   224→
   225→  info "Configuring PATH in .profile and .bashrc..."
   226→
   227→  # Add to .profile for SSH non-interactive shells
   228→  touch "$profile"
   229→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$profile"
   230→
   231→  if ! grep -qF "$path_export" "$profile" 2>/dev/null; then
   232→    echo "$path_export" >> "$profile"
   233→    wrote "$profile"
   234→  fi
   235→
   236→  # Add to .bashrc for interactive shells
   237→  touch "$bashrc"
   238→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$bashrc"
   239→
   240→  if ! grep -qF "$path_export" "$bashrc" 2>/dev/null; then
   241→    echo "$path_export" >> "$bashrc"
   242→    wrote "$bashrc"
   243→  fi
   244→
   245→  ok "PATH configured in .profile and .bashrc"
   246→}
   247→
   248→# ═══ install_prerequisites ════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   249→# Install required host tools: zstd (for backup/restore), rsync (for data copy)
   250→
   251→install_prerequisites() {
   252→  local missing=()
   253→  command -v zstd >/dev/null 2>&1  || missing+=("zstd")
   254→  command -v rsync >/dev/null 2>&1 || missing+=("rsync")
   255→  if [ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
   256→    info "Installing prerequisites: ${missing[*]}..."
   257→    apt-get install -y "${missing[@]}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
   258→      || warn "Failed to install ${missing[*]} — backup/restore may not work"
   259→    ok "Prerequisites installed: ${missing[*]}"
   260→  fi
   261→}
   262→
   263→# ═══ write_admin_script ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   264→# Install sandcastle-admin to $SANDCASTLE_HOME/bin/
   265→
   266→write_admin_script() {
   267→  local bin_dir="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/bin"
   268→  mkdir -p "$bin_dir"
   269→

   297→# ═══ ensure_dirs ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   298→# Create/fix data directories and ownership. Safe to run repeatedly.
   299→
   300→ensure_dirs() {
   301→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/etc
   302→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/bin
   303→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/{users,sandboxes,wetty,postgres,snapshots}
   304→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/traefik/{dynamic,certs}
   305→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"
   306→  # Own top-level data dirs (not -R: per-user subdirs are bind-mounted into
   307→  # Sysbox containers which use a different UID range via /etc/subuid).
   308→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_UID}:${SANDCASTLE_GID}" \
   309→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/users \
   310→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/sandboxes \
   311→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/snapshots \
   312→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/wetty
   313→  chown -R "${SANDCASTLE_UID}:${SANDCASTLE_GID}" \
   314→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/traefik/dynamic
   315→  # Per-user dirs: own the user-level parent, then chmod 777 the bind-mount
   316→  # targets so Sysbox-mapped root can write to them.
   317→  for d in "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/users/*; do
   318→    [ -d "$d" ] && chown "${SANDCASTLE_UID}:${SANDCASTLE_GID}" "$d"
   319→  done
   320→  for d in "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/users/*/home \
   321→           "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/users/*/data \
   322→           "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/sandboxes/*/vol; do
   323→    [ -d "$d" ] && chmod 777 "$d"
   324→  done
   325→  # Fix home dir permissions inside running sandbox containers so sshd
   326→  # StrictModes is satisfied (rejects 777 home dirs).
   327→  for cid in $($DOCKER ps -q 2>/dev/null); do
   328→    user=$($DOCKER inspect --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' "$cid" \
   329→      | grep '^SANDCASTLE_USER=' | cut -d= -f2 || true)
   330→    [ -n "$user" ] && $DOCKER exec "$cid" chmod 755 "/home/$user" 2>/dev/null || true
   331→  done
   332→  usermod -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME" "$SANDCASTLE_USER" 2>/dev/null || true
   333→  ok "Data directories verified"
   334→}

   336→# ═══ Helpers ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   337→
   338→show_image_info() {
   339→  local repo="$1"
   340→  python3 -c "
   341→import urllib.request, json, sys, re
   342→from datetime import datetime, timezone
   343→repo, arch = '${repo}', '${ARCH}'
   344→try:
   345→    r = urllib.request.urlopen(f'https://ghcr.io/token?scope=repository:thieso2/{repo}:pull')
   346→    token = json.load(r)['token']
   347→    h = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
   348→    req = urllib.request.Request(
   349→        f'https://ghcr.io/v2/thieso2/{repo}/manifests/latest',
   350→        headers={**h, 'Accept': 'application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json'})
   351→    m = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
   352→    if 'manifests' in m:
   353→        for p in m['manifests']:
   354→            if p.get('platform', {}).get('architecture') == arch:
   355→                req = urllib.request.Request(
   356→                    f'https://ghcr.io/v2/thieso2/{repo}/manifests/{p[\"digest\"]}',
   357→                    headers={**h, 'Accept': 'application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json'})
   358→                m = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
   359→                break
   360→    cfg = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(
   361→        urllib.request.Request(f'https://ghcr.io/v2/thieso2/{repo}/blobs/{m[\"config\"][\"digest\"]}', headers=h)))
   362→    ts = re.sub(r'(\.\d{6})\d+', r'\1', cfg['created']).replace('Z', '+00:00')
   363→    dt = datetime.fromisoformat(ts)
   364→    secs = int((datetime.now(timezone.utc) - dt).total_seconds())
   365→    if secs < 0: secs = 0
   366→    if secs >= 86400: ago = f'{secs // 86400}d ago'
   367→    elif secs >= 3600: ago = f'{secs // 3600}h ago'
   368→    else: ago = f'{max(1, secs // 60)}m ago'
   369→    print(f'  ghcr.io/thieso2/{repo}:latest    built {dt.strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC\")} ({ago})')
   370→except Exception:
   371→    print(f'  ghcr.io/thieso2/{repo}:latest    (unable to fetch build info)')
   372→" 2>/dev/null || echo "  ghcr.io/thieso2/${repo}:latest"
   373→}
   374→
   375→pick_private_net() {
   376→  # Pick a random 10.X.0.0/16 not already routed on this host.
   377→  local used_octets
   378→  used_octets=$(
   379→    { ip route 2>/dev/null; ip addr 2>/dev/null; } \
   380→      | grep -oE '10\.[0-9]+\.' \
   381→      | grep -oE '\.[0-9]+\.' \
   382→      | tr -d '.' \
   383→      | sort -un
   384→  )
   385→  for b in $(shuf -i 1-254 2>/dev/null || python3 -c "import random,sys; l=list(range(1,255)); random.shuffle(l); print('\n'.join(map(str,l)))"); do
   386→    if ! echo "$used_octets" | grep -qx "$b"; then
   387→      echo "10.${b}.0.0/16"
   388→      return
   389→    fi
   390→  done
   391→  # Fallback
   392→  echo "10.$(shuf -i 1-254 -n1 2>/dev/null || echo 89).0.0/16"
   393→}
   394→
   395→find_free_subnet() {
   396→  local used
   397→  used=$(
   398→    { ip route 2>/dev/null; ip addr 2>/dev/null; netstat -rn 2>/dev/null; } \
   399→      | grep -oE '(10|172|192)\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' \
   400→      | sort -un
   401→    $DOCKER network ls -q 2>/dev/null | while read -r nid; do
   402→      $DOCKER network inspect --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' "$nid" 2>/dev/null
   403→    done | grep -oE '(10|172|192)\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' \
   404→      | sort -un
   405→  )
   406→  for b in $(shuf -i 16-31); do
   407→    for c in $(shuf -i 1-254 | head -5); do
   408→      if ! echo "$used" | grep -q "^172\.${b}\.${c}\."; then
   409→        echo "172.${b}.${c}.0/24"
   410→        return
   411→      fi
   412→    done
   413→  done
   414→  for b in $(shuf -i 1-254 | head -20); do
   415→    for c in $(shuf -i 1-254 | head -5); do
   416→      if ! echo "$used" | grep -q "^10\.${b}\.${c}\."; then
   417→        echo "10.${b}.${c}.0/24"
   418→        return
   419→      fi
   420→    done
   421→  done
   422→  echo "172.30.99.0/24"
   423→}
   424→
   425→install_mkcert() {
   426→  if command -v mkcert &>/dev/null; then
   427→    return 0
   428→  fi
   429→  info "Installing mkcert..."
   430→  local arch
   431→  arch=$(uname -m)
   432→  local mkcert_arch
   433→  case "$arch" in
   434→    x86_64)  mkcert_arch="amd64" ;;
   435→    aarch64) mkcert_arch="arm64" ;;
   436→    *)       die "Unsupported architecture for mkcert: $arch" ;;
   437→  esac
   438→  curl -fsSL "https://dl.filippo.io/mkcert/latest?for=linux/${mkcert_arch}" \
   439→    -o /usr/local/bin/mkcert
   440→  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/mkcert
   441→  ok "mkcert installed"
   442→}
